title: Arab International Bank (AIB) (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Arab International Bank (AIB) in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Arab International Bank (AIB)?
faq.answer: Arab International Bank (AIB) is a major commercial bank operating in Egypt, providing comprehensive banking services to individuals, corporations, government agencies, and financial institutions. AIB is one of Egypt's most established and deeply rooted financial institutions with a strong focus on corporate banking alongside retail services. The bank operates an extensive network of 96 branches throughout Egypt and maintains three branches outside the country. AIB is known for its emphasis on corporate and institutional banking, trade finance, and international banking operations.

faq.question: When was Arab International Bank (AIB) founded?
faq.answer: Arab International Bank was established in 1974 by virtue of an international treaty concluded by the governments of the Arab Republic of Egypt, Libya, Sultanate of Oman, State of Qatar, and United Arab Emirates. The bank was created as a joint venture between these Arab states to facilitate economic development and foreign trade. The founding treaty established AIB's purpose as carrying out banking, financial, and commercial activities related to economic development projects and foreign trade, particularly for the benefit of the member states and other Arab countries. This unique multinational structure distinguished AIB as one of the first pan-Arab banking institutions. In 2012, AIB underwent significant expansion of its charter following an Extraordinary General Assembly meeting, allowing it to conduct transactions in Egyptian pounds while maintaining its international privileges and serving all currencies.

faq.question: Where is Arab International Bank (AIB) headquartered?
faq.answer: Arab International Bank is headquartered in Cairo, Egypt. The bank's legal domicile and principal offices are located in the Egyptian capital, which serves as the hub for its domestic and international operations. Cairo's position as Egypt's financial center makes it an ideal location for AIB's headquarters, providing access to government institutions, corporate clients, and the broader Egyptian banking sector.

faq.question: Where does Arab International Bank (AIB) operate?
faq.answer: Arab International Bank operates throughout Egypt with an extensive network of 96 branches distributed across major cities and economic centers. The bank also maintains three branches outside Egypt to support its international banking operations. Within Egypt, AIB's branches serve customers in Cairo, Alexandria, and other key provincial centers. The bank supports its branch network with more than 485 ATMs throughout the country, providing customers with convenient access to banking services nationwide.

faq.question: What services does Arab International Bank (AIB) offer?
faq.answer: AIB provides a comprehensive range of banking services across multiple segments: Corporate Banking: Project finance, syndicated loans, trade finance, foreign exchange services, inward and outward transfers, debt restructuring, acquisition financing, and treasury solutions. The bank specializes in serving large corporations, government entities, and institutional clients. Retail Banking: Personal banking accounts, savings accounts, time deposits, personal loans, credit cards, and debit cards. The bank offers wealth management services for individual clients. Trade Finance: Comprehensive trade finance solutions including letters of credit, documentary collections, and trade guarantees to support Egyptian businesses in international commerce. Treasury Services: Foreign exchange dealing room operations (AIB established Egypt's first international dealing room in 1982), money market operations, and financial derivatives. Investment Banking: Through its subsidiary Arab International Banking Company (SAIB), in which AIB holds 50.438%, the bank offers investment banking services. Digital Banking: Online banking platforms, mobile banking applications, and 24/7 customer service through dedicated contact centers (19555 domestically, +202 19555 internationally). The bank has undergone digital transformation including digitization of customer files and automation of KYC processes. Specialized Services: AIB upgraded branches to serve customers with special needs, providing accessible banking experiences aligned with Central Bank of Egypt initiatives.

faq.question: What makes Arab International Bank (AIB) unique?
faq.answer: AIB's establishment through an international treaty among five Arab governments gives it a distinctive multinational character. The bank's shareholders include the Central Bank of Egypt (38.76%), Libyan Foreign Bank (38.76%), Abu Dhabi Investment Authority (12.503%), Qatar Holding Company (4.984%), Oman Investment Authority (2.49%), and International Capital Trading Co. (2.503%). AIB pioneered several innovations in Egyptian banking, including launching Egypt's first international dealing room in 1982 and introducing the first credit cards to the Egyptian market. The bank maintains strong international capabilities that enable it to serve as a bridge between Egyptian businesses and global markets. The bank has invested significantly in digital transformation, partnering with Iron Mountain to digitize over 3 million backfiles for enhanced customer experiences and automated workflows. AIB has also developed partnerships with major international banking institutions through its correspondent banking network.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Arab International Bank (AIB)?
faq.answer: Arab International Bank (AIB) is a commercial bank operating in Egypt with a strong emphasis on corporate and institutional banking. The bank is regulated by the Central Bank of Egypt and operates under a special charter established by international treaty. AIB's unique legal structure as a bank established by international treaty gives it certain privileges while maintaining full compliance with Egyptian banking regulations. The bank is licensed to conduct comprehensive banking operations including accepting deposits, providing loans, facilitating trade finance, and offering treasury services to both domestic and international clients across all major currencies including the Egyptian pound.
